description: One of China's three major museums, featuring a rich collection and six exhibition halls including the History Hall, Art Hall, and Republic of China Hall.
Nanjing's premier shopping center, showcasing internationally renowned brands, located in the heart of the Xinjiekou commercial district.
Qinhuai River sightseeing experience, offering views of ancient architecture and nightscapes along the riverbanks, capturing the charm of the Six Dynasties capital.
A cluster of Republic of China-era mansions, a well-preserved historic district with elegant architectural styles.
A protected district of Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, blending traditional residences with modern commerce, offering a taste of old Nanjing charm.
A double-helix scenic pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark with views of the river and city skyline.
The mausoleum of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, a memorial site for the founding father of the Republic of China, and a central attraction in the Zhongshan Scenic Area.
Nanjing's famous food street, featuring a variety of local snacks and night market delicacies, offering an authentic taste of Nanjing.

attraction_name: Nanjing City Wall Taicheng Scenic Area
description: The best-preserved section of the Ming Dynasty city wall, where visitors can climb and enjoy panoramic views of Xuanwu Lake.
A double-deck railway and highway bridge independently designed and built in China, serving as a city landmark.
